rulings.Case Title: VICTOR TUNG VS. AMI QI et al
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday July 1, 2016, line 1. PLAINTIFF VICTOR TUNG MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION DENIED. Plaintiff seeks rescission as to defendant Ami Qi under Civil Code §1689. (Memo. 1:10-11). However, as plaintiff concedes, that statute's plain language requires, at a minimum, "connivance of the party as to whom he rescinds" (id. at 4:9), and plaintiff's separate statement is bereft of evidence that defendant Qi was anything but innocent of connivance. Well-established California law is, as plaintiff also concedes, that "the innocent principal is not charged with the common agent's misconduct." Memo. 13:21-22, citing Herndan v. Hanson (1920) 182 Cal. 538, 545. Further, actions of an agent are not imputed to the principal when outside the scope of the agent's authority. Redman v. Walters (1979) 88 Cal.App.3d 448, 454. The authorities plaintiff cites are distinguishable and/or fail to address Civil Code §1689's plain language requiring "connivance." = (501/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: PNC BANK, NA VS. NELLY SERVELLON ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday July 1, 2016, line 2. PLAINTIFF PNC BANK, NA MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OFF CALENDAR. No courtesy copies of the moving papers received as of 8:00 a.m. on June 30, 2016. = (501/RBU)
